entries.position_summary: The United States commits not to use or threaten nuclear weapons against NPT-compliant non-nuclear-weapon states, offering policy-based negative security assurances and legally binding assurances through protocols to nuclear-weapon-free zones. It underscores the importance of these assurances to non-nuclear-weapon states.
entries.quote: The United States has long maintained a negative security assurance as part of its nuclear doctrine. The United States will not use or threaten to use nuclear weapons against non-nuclear weapon states that are party to the NPT and in compliance with their nuclear nonproliferation obligations. This describes, and therefore is applied to, the vast majority of countries. These assurances are a matter of U.S. policy, but we also offer legally binding negative security assurances in connection with the Protocols to the nuclear-weapon-free zones. We understand the importance to non-nuclear weapon states of such negative security assurances.

entries.position_summary: The United States reaffirms its negative security assurance: it will not use or threaten to use nuclear weapons against NPT non-nuclear-weapon states in compliance with their nonproliferation obligations. This commitment is reiterated in the U.S. Nuclear Posture Review.
entries.quote: • First, as reaffirmed in our Nuclear Posture Review, the United States maintains a “negative security assurance” that it will not use or threaten to use nuclear weapons against non-nuclear-weapon states that are party to the NPT and in compliance with their nuclear non-proliferation obligations.

entries.position_summary: The United States reaffirms its positive security assurance under UNSCR 984, committing to provide or support immediate assistance to any NPT non-nuclear-weapon state that is a victim of, or threatened with, nuclear aggression, including radiological response, detection/assessment, and environmental mitigation. The United States, France, and the United Kingdom jointly reaffirmed these assurances at the Tenth NPT Review Conference in August 2022.
entries.quote: The United States also continues to adhere to a positive security assurance, as noted in UN Security Council Resolution 984, to provide or support immediate assistance to any non-nuclear-weapon State Party to the NPT that is a victim of an act of, or an object of a threat of, aggression in which nuclear weapons are used. Assistance may include radiological emergency response, detection and assessment, and environmental mitigation. The United States, along with France and the United Kingdom, reaffirmed these principles in a statement at the Tenth NPT Review Conference on August 4, 2022.

entries.position_summary: The statement highlights and supports the P3 (France, UK, US) joint reaffirmation that non-nuclear-weapon States Parties have a legitimate interest in receiving security assurances and that the P3 recognize and reaffirm providing such assurances. It signals endorsement of strengthening assurances to safeguard NNWS security under the NPT.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, I would also like to call to your attention to the P3 Joint Statement on Security Assurances issued at the Tenth NPT Review Conference on August 4, 2022. In this statement, the governments of France, the United Kingdom and the United States recognized the legitimate interest of non-nuclear-weapon States Parties to the NPT in receiving certain assurances to safeguard their security. Accordingly, the three states reaffirmed the following:

entries.position_summary: The United States supports regional Nuclear-Weapon-Free Zones, formed by consensus among all states in the region, as the most practical means to provide legally binding negative security assurances. This reflects a longstanding U.S. policy of extending NSAs primarily through NWFZ treaty protocols.
entries.quote: Consistent with long-standing U.S. policy, we have long believed that regional nuclear weapon free zones, established on the basis of arrangements freely arrived at among all States of the region concerned, provide the most expeditious and practical way to extend legally binding negative security assurances.

entries.position_summary: The country supports establishing regional nuclear-weapon-free zones through consensual regional arrangements, viewing them as the most effective and practical means to provide legally binding negative security assurances. It favors NWFZs as the preferred vehicle for extending such guarantees.
entries.quote: known complications in such negotiations, we have long believed that regional nuclear-weapon-free zones (NWFZs), established on the basis of arrangements freely arrived at among all States of the region concerned, provide the most expeditious and practical way to extend legally binding negative security assurances.

entries.position_summary: The United States reaffirms its positive security assurance under UNSCR 984 to provide or support immediate assistance to any NPT non‑nuclear‑weapon State that is a victim of nuclear weapon use or threat of use. This assistance can include radiological emergency response, detection and assessment, and environmental mitigation.
entries.quote: The United States also continues to adhere to a positive security assurance, as noted in UN Security Council Resolution 984, to provide or support immediate assistance to any non-nuclear-weapon State Party to the NPT that is a “victim of an act of, or an object of a threat of, aggression in which nuclear weapons are used.” Assistance may include radiological emergency response, detection and assessment, and environmental mitigation.

entries.position_summary: The United States reaffirms its positive security assurance as referenced in UN Security Council Resolution 984. It commits to seek immediate Security Council action to assist NPT non-nuclear-weapon States if they are threatened with or subjected to nuclear aggression.
entries.quote: • Third, the United States recalls its positive security assurance as noted in UN Security Council resolution 984.

entries.position_summary: The country urges nuclear-weapon states to uphold and reaffirm their existing negative security assurances not to use or threaten to use nuclear weapons against NPT non-nuclear-weapon states.
entries.quote: • First, their existing security assurances regarding the use or threat of use of nuclear weapons against NNWS Parties to the NPT.

entries.position_summary: The country supports arms control engagement, risk reduction measures, and advancing a fissile material cutoff treaty to strengthen the NPT. It also stresses the importance of negative security assurances and calls on all states to uphold the assurances they have given.
entries.quote: Steps such as these – on arms control engagement, on risk reduction, and on Cutoff – can help strengthen the NPT’s role as a positive force for restraint and diplomacy. So too do negative security assurances, and we call on all states to uphold the assurances they have given.
